observations: L5-L17: the opening theory run was the chapter's main texture gap; connective tissue added there moved participial density from 4.9% to 9.1% and comma:period from 0.557 to 0.636.
L35-L39: Sylvanas's exchange now earns its short beats by leaning on longer setup clauses before the clipped replies.
L89: the camp-entry read now carries Jaina's scan in a built sentence ('read as she walked, counting wounded') instead of stacked notes.
L141: Jaina's rebuttal to Arthas keeps the short declarative edge, but the anger is grounded in an action beat rather than left as bare statement.
L221-L243: the trader scene now carries tracing / having / hearsay connective tissue, softening the old telegram chain without blunting suspicion.

Post-fix rhythm metrics: mean sentence length 11.7, CV 0.827, short-sentence share 39.0%, long-sentence share 19.1%; opener entropy and one-sentence-paragraph rate still sit outside target, but the remaining variance reads intentional by ear rather than machine-flat.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.default / textureMetrics.participial_pct — add connective tissue to analytic stack
fixes.before: Grain first. Grain from the Barov-side feeder roads moving through lanes already thick with wagon traffic and mill work.
fixes.after: Grain first: grain from the Barov-side feeder roads moving through lanes already thick with wagon traffic and mill work... turning the thing on the road into household routine.

fixes.before: She had felt it at the Crossbar and at Millbrook Spur. Here it was thicker. The basin was ahead.
fixes.after: She had felt it at the Crossbar and at Millbrook Spur, reading the same pressure off the soil. Here it was thicker. The basin was ahead...

fixes.rule: textureMetrics.participial_pct — distribute ,Ving phrases outside dialogue blocks
fixes.before: Cyndia rode ahead. Lyriel kept the back trail.
fixes.after: Cyndia rode ahead, keeping the lead without needing the word. Lyriel kept the back trail, watching the damp road vanish behind them.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.emphasisRule — support clipped Sylvanas reply with fuller lead-in
fixes.before: Sylvanas turned her head once. "I know."
fixes.after: Sylvanas turned her head once, glancing over. "I know."

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.default — replace note-stack rhythm with built observation
fixes.before: Jaina read as she walked. Wounded, but not in numbers that spoke of collapse.
fixes.after: Jaina read as she walked, counting wounded but not in numbers that spoke of collapse.

fixes.before: Jaina drew the wards closer. The wrongness pressed back.
fixes.after: Jaina drew the wards closer, bracing against the push. The wrongness pressed back.

observations: No therapy-speak, corporate diction, or modern colloquial register survived in narration or dialogue.
L9 now names the last step as 'a summons' rather than the vague placeholder 'something,' preserving uncertainty without defaulting to AI haze.
L111 grounds the command tent in 'broth' instead of 'something hot,' which is more specific and less generic-documental.
L197 now gives Jaina 'proof he can act on' instead of 'something he can act on,' tightening her field argument into usable terms.
L215 replaces 'something narrower and meaner' with 'an ache narrower and meaner,' keeping the sensation embodied instead of gestured at.
The only remaining -ly temporal softener is 'finally' at L7; here it marks earned accumulation rather than dead-air padding.

fixes.before: At death, something called and the prepared body answered...
fixes.after: At death, a summons came and the prepared body answered...

fixes.before: two cups of something hot waited on a stand by the entrance
fixes.after: two cups of broth waited on a stand by the entrance

fixes.before: we get something he can act on
fixes.after: we get proof he can act on

fixes.before: but something narrower and meaner, needling from behind the right eye
fixes.after: but an ache narrower and meaner, needling from behind the right eye

fixes.rule: document-voice cleanup — reduce abstract reporting diction
fixes.before: filed in Jaina's head secondhand and none too securely
fixes.after: filed in Jaina's head by hearsay and none too firmly

observations: Metaphor density stays low at roughly 0.9 per 1000 words, inside the global 1-3/1000 guidance and close to Jaina's low-moderate allowance.
L11's 'Death closes the lock' is process/mechanism imagery, which fits Jaina's analytic fingerprint.
L13's feeder roads 'knitting themselves' into a system uses structural/process language rather than generic lyricism.
L41's sealed questions / stone doors imagery stays tied to scholarship and prohibition, not to ornamental prettiness.
No forbidden storm, burden, drowning, shattered, or light/dark moral metaphors appear.
No paragraph stacks more than one metaphor, and no consecutive sentences lean on separate image systems.
L87-L90: the camp overview had drifted over the 100-word ceiling; splitting it restored the guideline without thinning detail.
Maximum paragraph length now lands at 100 words; most narration paragraphs sit in the 40-95 word band.
Scene transitions all use preferred methods: hard cut after road analysis, causal carryover into Strahnbrad, and planted-question arrival into Andorhal.
No banned temporal bridge phrases ('Later that evening,' 'The next morning,' 'Hours passed') appear anywhere in the chapter.

One-sentence paragraph rate remains 44.4%, well above the numeric range; most of that inflation comes from dialogue and beat-paragraph shaping, so I left the strongest landings in place rather than flattening them into bricks.

fixes.before: The camp had gone up fast ... The rest of the sprawl belonged to Lordaeron...
fixes.after: The camp had gone up fast ... Men moved through it with the blunt rhythm of soldiers who had stopped noticing the cold. [paragraph break] The forge she had heard from the road sat somewhere beyond the eastern tree line...

observations: Tagging remains plain where tags appear: 25 'said', 4 'asked', and 1 permitted 'called'; no banned murmured / breathed / hissed / adverbial tags survive.
L141, L175, L179, and L203 use action beats that identify speaker while also advancing movement or posture, matching the action-beat double-duty rule.
The trader-pattern exchange at L247-L257 had drifted into an overlong untagged chain; added light tags now keep turn order unmistakable.
The records exchange at L193-L201 also got one extra tag, preventing the short-line cluster from reading like floating text.

Chapter-wide untagged percentage is still high because the chapter is dialogue-light overall and many exchanges are intentionally rapid two-person volleys; the most confusing chains were corrected rather than globally over-tagged.

fixes.after: "And now it's odd twice," Jaina said.

observations: The opening moves in Jaina's preferred sequence—claim, revision, consequence—rather than in generic suspense beats.
Mean sentence length of 11.7 sits inside Jaina's 10-16 word default, with pressure compression at Strahnbrad and the Andorhal approach.
Jaina notices magical and logistical pattern before social texture: grain routes, ward pressure, loading yards, flour sweetness, signed books.
Emotional register stays controlled and clinical; Arthas hits as bodily sting, revised expectation, and operational consequence rather than lyrical confession.
Filter verbs remain sparse and mostly profession-justified (three uses of 'felt,' all tied to magical sensing rather than generic perception).
Sylvanas's dialogue inside Jaina POV stays clipped, tactical, and deadpan; it never slides into explanatory softness.
Adverb load is 15 across ~4420 words, or about 1.7 per 500-word page—inside the max-2-per-page floor.
Passive-voice heuristic lands at roughly 1.6% of sentences, comfortably under the 8% cap.
Filter-word sweep found no noticed / observed / registered / realized constructions; the chapter stays direct on perception.
Simile load stays controlled at three 'like' uses and two 'as if' uses, inside the chapter cap.
'Something' dropped to zero after the cleanup passes, eliminating one of the main anti-slop quality-floor risks.

fixes.rule: proseQualityFloor / anti-slop — trim unnecessary adverbial shading

fixes.before: 'something' placeholders remained in key reasoning beats
fixes.after: all 'something' placeholders were removed from the chapter

observations: Exposition integration passes: plague mechanics arrive through Jaina's active problem-solving, Arthas briefing, and sensory contact with the road rather than narrator-aside dump.
Power dynamics pass: Arthas gets the shorter declaratives ('It makes the choice mine'), Jaina gets longer argued lines, and Sylvanas's post-scene diagnosis compresses into hard tactical read.
Scene shape passes: the chapter opens in medias res on analysis, turns at Arthas's refusal, and lands on the no-and disaster of a functioning Andorhal.
Speculative integration passes: wards, taint, contamination, and undeath read as infrastructure and logistics, not spectacle.
Subtext density is strong in the Arthas scene; they argue over troop allocation while the older wound—whether he can ever hear her in time—does the secondary work.
Action-pacing rules are N/A for combat, but the approach beats still prioritize tactile strain, hoof response, jaw tension, and ward pressure over visual fireworks.
Humor policy holds: only one dry Sylvanas line about spinning straw into gold survives, and it reads as bitter professional wit rather than banter.

fixes.before: He heard sickness and handed it to his healers. The orcs stayed with the soldiers.
fixes.after: He heard sickness and handed it to his healers, keeping the orcs with the soldiers.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — field-academic rather than lab-report; vague placeholders tightened at L9, L111, L197, and L215.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass with numeric note — 11.7-word mean and pressure compression fit Jaina, though one-sentence paragraph rate stays high because of dialogue formatting.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — ~0.9/1000 words, using process/mechanism imagery rather than generic lyric imagery.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — Arthas scenes render hurt through body, revision, and restraint, not melodrama.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — opening and arrival both run observation -> inference -> next test in Jaina's hypothesis-driven mode.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — ward pressure, wrongness, flour sweetness, route charts, and loading yards dominate over beauty description.
fingerprintChecks.humorType: pass — dry wit is sparse and self-controlled; no quippy performance under pressure.
fingerprintChecks.powerPosture: pass — baseline analytical posture shifts to shorter, harder lines only when Arthas forces the issue.
fingerprintChecks.speculativeComfort: pass — plague and wards are treated as professional problems, not wonders.
fingerprintChecks.expositionFilter: pass — she explains only what she is actively modeling or what Arthas needs in the room.

ruleViolations: L5-L17 originally compressed Jaina's analytic voice into too many hard stops; revised toward built reasoning sentences.
L9/L111/L197/L215 originally relied on generic placeholders where Jaina should have named the phenomenon or object more exactly.
Chapter-wide one-sentence paragraph rate (44.4%) remains above the numeric target, but the excess comes from deliberate beat-and-dialogue formatting rather than out-of-character flattening.

metrics.dialogueToNarrationRatio: 20:80 chapter-wide; scene mix skews travel/briefing heavy, so this sits below the procedural-chapter target without being repairable by no-new-content editing.

flaggedZones.instruction: If later passes trim here again, preserve at least one participial link and one compound sentence in the opening theory sequence instead of breaking every inference into isolated statements.
In the Arthas briefing setup, keep connective clauses attached to the command-reading beats so the room does not collapse into report fragments.
Protect the action beat under Jaina's rebuttal; do not strip the bracing / table-edge construction back into bare declaratives.
In the trader exchange, preserve the tracing / having connective phrases and avoid snapping the directions back into isolated road-note sentences.
The repeated-farewell exchange should keep at least one spoken-line attribution and one connective clause so the clue lands as conversation, not caption fragments.

textureFindings.priorityInstruction: Texture now passes; if later edits compress the chapter again, protect connective tissue first in the opening analysis, Arthas briefing, and repeated-farewell exchange.
